agent_outputs.raw: The fairness claim is unsubstantiated. Macro-F1 on historical decisions measures predictive accuracy, not subgroup fairness. No fairness criterion is specified and no gender-stratified metrics are provided. The historical training labels (manager recommendations approved by committee) are not a neutral target — both the manager recommendation stage and the committee approval stage could be sources of gender disparity. The model may replicate these disparities more consistently. The evidence as presented cannot support a claim that the model is fairer across gender groups.

The proposal's fairness claim requires three things that are absent: a specified fairness criterion, subgroup-level metrics, and an audit of the historical training labels for gender disparity. Macro-F1 does not measure any of these. The temporal holdout and name removal are appropriate practices for a high-quality predictive model, but they are not fairness evaluations. Without the missing evidence, the claim 'fairer across gender groups' has no measurable basis. Critique wins.

The evaluation does not support the fairness claim. Three problems: (1) macro-F1 is an accuracy metric, not a fairness metric; (2) no subgroup analysis or fairness criterion specification is provided; (3) historical promotion labels may encode the original process's gender bias. A model trained to match historically biased decisions is not fairer — it systematizes the bias. The name removal and temporal holdout are good practices for a different purpose. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. All three agents identify the three core issues. The valid practices (name removal, temporal holdout) are correctly exonerated as insufficient for a fairness claim.

etd_constraint.condition: Specify fairness criterion; compute gender-stratified true positive and false negative rates for model and historical process; audit historical labels for gender disparity controlling for job level, tenure, performance
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Historical labels show gender disparity controlling for observables, or model shows worse subgroup error rates under specified criterion
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: No historical label disparity; model shows equal or improved subgroup outcomes under specified criterion
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: Mixed results across fairness criteria or subgroups

all_issues_raised: Macro-F1 is accuracy not fairness (all three agents)
No subgroup analysis or specified criterion (all three agents)
Historical labels may embed bias (all three agents)
